data.title: SANUWAVE Health GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.10, revenue of $10.2M beats by $0.06M

data.title: Tempus AI files mixed-securities shelf
data.description:
• Tempus AI (NASDAQ:TEM) has filed a registration statement with the SEC for a proposed sale of securities.
  • The registration statement contains three prospectuses: a base prospectus that covers the sale of mixed securities; a prospectus that covers the sale by selling stockholders of Class A common stock; and a sales agreement prospectus, which covers the sale of up to $500M of Tempus AI’s Class A common stock under a controlled equity offering.
  • TEM +7.7% premarket.

data.title: V2X announces sale of shares by Vertex Aerospace
data.description:
V2X (NYSE:VVX) announced the sale of 2 million shares of its common stock by Vertex Aerospace Holdco.

The company is not selling any shares in this offering and will not receive any proceeds. The offering is expected to close around August 11, 2025, pending standard closing conditions.

RBC Capital Markets is acting as the sole underwriter for the offering.

Subject to the closing of the offering, V2X has agreed to purchase from the underwriter 200,000 of the shares of its common stock that are subject to the offering at a price per share equal to the price to be paid to Vertex Aerospace by RBC Capital Markets.

V2X intends to fund the repurchase of its common stock with cash on hand.

Vertex Aerospace will continue to beneficially own 10,167,286 shares, or ~32.3% of V2X's outstanding common stock, after giving effect to the offering, including V2X's repurchase of shares of its common stock.

VVX -1.56% premarket.

data.title: GSK, CureVac to receive $740M as COVID vaccine patent dispute with Pfizer, BioNTech settled
data.description:
CureVac (NASDAQ:CVAC) announced on Friday that the company and its partner, GSK (NYSE:GSK), are set to receive $740M and other payments, as Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) have agreed to resolve a patent dispute related to messenger-RNA-based COVID vaccines in the U.S.

The settlement will “resolve and dismiss all pending patent litigation in the United States between the companies related to mRNA-based COVID-19 vaccines,” the German vaccine maker said.

It also outlines a plan to resolve ex-U.S. patent disputes subject to the completion of BioNTech’s (NASDAQ:BNTX) recently announced acquisition of CureVac (NASDAQ:CVAC).

According to the deal terms, CureVac (NASDAQ:CVAC) and GSK (NYSE:GSK) are eligible for a $740M payment and single-digit royalties on future U.S. sales of COVID-19 vaccines developed by BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) and Pfizer (NYSE:PFE).

In exchange, BioNTech (BNTX) and Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) will receive a non-exclusive license related to the sale, production, and import of mRNA-based COVID and influenza products in the U.S. It will be a global non-exclusive license pending BioNTech’s (BNTX) acquisition of CureVac (CVAC).

Having partnered with CureVac (CVAC) in 2020 to develop mRNA vaccines for infectious diseases, GSK (NYSE:GSK) announced that it would receive a $370M upfront payment as part of the settlement.

Additionally, starting from 2025, the British drugmaker will be eligible for a 1% royalty related to U.S. sales of influenza, COVID-19, and related combination mRNA vaccine products developed by BioNTech (BNTX) and Pfizer (PFE).

Once the ex-U.S. patent litigations are settled, the company will receive a $130M additional payment and a similar royalty rate related to the sale of mRNA-based products developed by BioNTech (BNTX) and Pfizer (PFE) outside the U.S.

GSK (GSK) expects to record the settlement amounts in Q3 2025 under the other operating income. The royalty income will be recorded under total and core results in the income statements. In connection with the deal, GSK (GSK) has agreed to tender nearly 16.6M CureVac (CVAC) shares as part of BNTX’s buyout offer.

data.title: Sylvamo Non-GAAP EPS of $0.37 misses by $0.04, revenue of $794M misses by $32.07M
data.description:
• Sylvamo press release (NYSE:SLVM): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.37 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $794M (-14.9% Y/Y) misses by $32.07M.

  • Third Quarter Outlook

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $145 million to $165 million
    • Compared to the second quarter:
      • Price and mix are expected to decrease by $15 million to $20 million due to paper and pulp prices in Europe
      • Volume is projected to improve in the range of $15 million to $20 million, primarily due to seasonality in Latin America and North America
      • Operations and other costs are expected to be favorable by up to $5 million, primarily due to improving operational performance
      • Input and transportation costs are projected to be stable in the range of $(5) million to $5 million
      • Total planned maintenance outage expenses will decrease by $66 million with no outages planned in the quarter
    • We expect quarterly earnings to significantly improve in the second half of the year as we benefit from much lower planned maintenance outage expenses, improving volumes and better operations.

data.title: IAS raises 2025 revenue outlook to up to $605M as CTV and AI investments fuel growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Integral Ad Science (IAS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lisa Utzschneider opened the call by highlighting continued business momentum and profitable growth in the second quarter, stating "Total revenue increased 16% at a 35% adjusted EBITDA margin." She announced an increased full year outlook, attributing the raise to strong Q2 performance, ongoing AI investments, and product innovation, especially in performance, reach, and technology partnerships.
  • Utzschneider detailed the expansion of CTV offerings and global reach, emphasizing, "In the second quarter, we reached a 2-year expansion and renewal with Samsung, a leader in the CTV space and our largest OEM partner." She also referenced new international wins, including a major German publisher and Australia SBS for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
  • She highlighted advancements in AI capabilities, noting "Up to 97% of model validation has moved from humans in the loop to Gen AI. Our AI labeling is now 29x faster and 45% more precise than human annotators."
  • Utzschneider announced the appointment of Care Seifer as Executive Vice President of Global Sales and welcomed Alpana Wegner as CFO.
  • CFO Alpana Wegner stated, "Total revenue in the second quarter increased 16% to $149 million, ahead of our prior outlook as a result of the higher-than-expected spend on our social measurement offerings and optimization products."

Outlook

  • Wegner provided updated guidance, stating "For the third quarter ending September 30, 2025, we expect total revenue in the range of $148 million to $150 million or 12% year-over-year growth at the midpoint. Adjusted EBITDA for the third quarter is expected in the range of $51 million to $53 million or a 35% margin at the midpoint."
  • Full-year 2025 guidance was raised: "We are increasing our revenue outlook to $597 million to $605 million or 13% year-over-year growth at the midpoint. For the full year 2025, we are increasing our adjusted EBITDA outlook to $208 million to $214 million or a 35% margin at the midpoint."
  • Gross margin for the full year is expected to be in the range of 77% to 79%, and the effective tax rate is projected at approximately 25%.

Financial Results

  • IAS reported Q2 revenue of $149 million, reflecting 16% year-over-year growth and a 35% adjusted EBITDA margin. Net income was $16 million or $0.10 per share, up from $8 million or $0.05 per share a year earlier.
  • Optimization revenue rose 16% to $68 million, while measurement revenue grew 8% to $57 million. Publisher revenue increased 36% to $24 million.
  • Social media revenue grew 22% and constituted 60% of measurement revenue and 23% of total revenue. Video represented 61% of measurement revenue with 26% growth.
  • International revenue increased 8% to $43 million, accounting for 30% of total revenue. The company reported $55 million in operating cash flow and cash and cash equivalents of $91 million at quarter end.
  • IAS paid off remaining long-term debt and announced an extension of its $300 million credit facility, allowing for borrowings up to $550 million.

Q&A

  • Andrew Jordan Marok, Raymond James: Asked about progress with Oracle (Moat) customer onboarding and upselling. Utzschneider explained that "we continue to see great momentum with our Oracle customers...very focused on onboarding, integrating the new brands, publishers and platforms to IAS." She also noted strong performance in optimization and ongoing cross-sell and upsell efforts.
  • Marok followed up on Social Optimization adoption. Utzschneider said, "We've doubled the number of advertisers who've adopted Prebid Social and where we're seeing lots of adoption happening is in EMEA." She also referenced a new Meta partnership to measure ThreadsFeed inventory.
  • James Edward Heaney, Jefferies: Inquired about publisher segment growth. Utzschneider pointed to "expanding our OEM partnerships including driving adoption of new CTV products, including Vault...We've also benefited in Publica."
  • Nick Gruner (for Youssef Squali), Truist: Asked for Q3 guidance breakdown and international trends. Wegner detailed expectations for high single-digit measurement growth, mid-teens optimization, and double-digit publisher growth. Utzschneider identified strong TMQ adoption in EMEA and APAC and continued beta activity in China.
  • Jason Helfstein, Oppenheimer: Queried measurement and optimization volatility. Utzschneider stated, "Measurement, yes, it's single digit at 8%, but it doubled quarter-over-quarter."
  • Unidentified Analyst (Key): Asked about optimization pricing and social adoption. Utzschneider said, "Our optimization pricing, it increased 10% year-over-year in Q2, primarily related to the ongoing adoption of QSP." She added, "We have more than doubled the number of brands on Meta who are adopting Prebid Social from Q1 to Q2."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on adoption rates, international expansion, and segment growth, with a generally positive but probing tone regarding sustainability and drivers of growth.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases such as "we are really pleased" and "we're excited about the ongoing strength." The tone was consistent with the prior quarter, though with heightened confidence due to increased guidance and successful execution.
  • Management’s tone was upbeat and confident in both quarters, while analysts remained constructive but attentive to volatility and adoption metrics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year revenue and adjusted EBITDA was raised compared to Q1, reflecting higher-than-expected Q2 performance.
  • Segment growth drivers remained similar: CTV, social, and optimization products drove growth in both quarters, though Q2 saw acceleration in international CTV wins and new product launches.
  • Management’s tone was consistently positive, with increased confidence in AI-driven execution and new leadership appointments in Q2.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on performance, volatility in business lines, and international expansion, with recurring questions on Oracle customer integration and social optimization.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing investments in infrastructure and higher costs related to optimization growth, but expects gross margin improvement in the second half.
  • Analyst questions probed segment volatility and the sustainability of growth, especially regarding international markets and product adoption rates.
  • No specific new business risks or macroeconomic threats were highlighted as material changes from previous quarters.

Final Takeaway

IAS delivered another quarter of robust growth, raising its full-year 2025 revenue outlook to up to $605 million and adjusted EBITDA to up to $214 million, driven by strong adoption of CTV and social products, expanding international footprint, and AI-powered innovation. The company highlighted major new partnerships, growing customer wins, and significant operational improvements, positioning itself for continued profitable expansion through the remainder of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Dominion Lending Centres Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 in-line, revenue of $24.6M beats by $1.87M
data.description:
• Dominion Lending Centres press release (TSX:DLCG:CA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 in-line.
  • Revenue of $24.6M (+30.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.87M.
  • Funded mortgage volumes ("FMV") grew 25% to $21.1 billion over Q2 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA rose 48% to $12.6 million and EBITDA margins expanded to 51% from 45% in Q2 2024.
  • Adoption of Velocity across the DLC Group broker network continued to expand, increasing to 82% from 72% in Q2 2024.
  • Increased the quarterly dividend by 33% to $0.04 per share or $0.16 annualized from $0.12 previously.

data.title: Solventum raises 2025 EPS guidance to $5.80-$5.95 with sales growth outlook up to 3% as transformation gains momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Solventum Corporation (SOLV) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bryan C. Hanson stated that "we are continuing where we left off in Q1, delivering another solid quarter. And as a result of this positive momentum and strong execution, we are raising our sales growth and EPS guidance for the year." He also emphasized that the company is "quickly and importantly, decisively building on the strong foundation established by 3M," and is "on a clear path to achieve our long-range plan commitments."
  • Hanson highlighted that the company's innovation process revamp is "on track," with a "forecasted new product pipeline...steadily increasing in value." He confirmed that after closing the Purification & Filtration (P&F) transaction, Solventum plans to focus on "disciplined tuck-in M&A to further enhance our progress."
  • The CEO provided updates on business segments: MedSurg showed progress with expanded V.A.C. Peel and Place launch in Europe, several large customer wins in negative pressure wound therapy, and strong demand for Tegaderm antimicrobial solutions. Dental Solutions gained momentum with product launches like Clinpro Clear and Filtek Easy Match, and Health Information Systems (HIS) saw continued expansion, including a new partnership with Ensemble in revenue cycle management.
  • Hanson noted, "Thermo has been a great partner," regarding the ongoing P&F business transaction, and that retaining the drinking water business "increases the opportunity to accelerate the close" and unlock additional value.
  • CFO Wayde D. McMillan said, "We’re pleased to be able to report another solid quarter as we navigate the separation from 3M and prepare for the divestiture of Purification and Filtration, all while gaining momentum in the business amidst an uncertain macro environment backdrop."

Outlook

  • Full year organic sales growth guidance was raised to a range of 2% to 3%, a 50 basis point increase above prior guidance. Excluding planned SKU exits, annual growth outlook is 2.5% to 3.5%.
  • Earnings per share guidance increased to a range of $5.80 to $5.95 from the previous guidance of $5.45 to $5.65. Hanson stated that this reflects "the strong performance in the quarter, combined with further improvements into the second half of the year."
  • Foreign exchange is now estimated to have a favorable 50 basis point impact on sales growth for the full year.
  • The estimated tariff impact for 2025 was reduced to $60 million to $80 million, down from the initial range of $80 million to $100 million.
  • Free cash flow guidance for the year remains at $450 million to $550 million.

Financial Results

  • Q2 sales were $2.2 billion, up 2.8% on an organic basis and 3.9% on a reported basis. Volume was the main driver of growth.
  • MedSurg delivered $1.2 billion in sales, up 3.9% organically, led by Infection Prevention and Surgical Solutions with 5.9% growth. Advanced Wound Care grew 0.8%.
  • Dental Solutions segment delivered $338 million in sales, up 70 basis points organically; Health Information Systems contributed $339 million, up 3.2% organically; Purification and Filtration delivered $252 million, up 3.1% organically.
  • Gross margins were 56% of sales, improving 20 basis points over prior year. Operating expenses were $736 million. Adjusted operating income was $474 million, with an operating margin of 21.9%.
  • Earnings per share for the quarter were $1.69. Solventum ended the quarter with $492 million in cash and equivalents.
  • $59 million in free cash flow was generated in Q2.

Q&A

  • Jason M. Bednar, Piper Sandler, asked about MedSurg growth trajectory and back half expectations. Hanson responded, "the commercial restructuring that we've done to specialize the team...is paying significant dividends," and noted, "the momentum is there. The confidence is growing, and I would expect to see us continue to grow ahead of where we are today."
  • Bednar also inquired about the EPS bridge. McMillan explained, "We're tightening the range as we progress through the year...we are only halfway through the year and...these variables can turn, we are comfortable with the raise that we put in place this quarter."
  • Travis Lee Steed, BofA Securities, asked about order timing, recalls, ERP, and SKU rationalization impact on growth. Hanson said, "the 2.8% that we posted is a good view of what the realistic growth was in the quarter."
  • Patrick Wood, Morgan Stanley, queried about capital allocation and M&A. Hanson stated, "the M&A to me is an enhancement to not a requirement to get to the mid-single-digit growth rate."
  • Frederick Allen Wise, Stifel, asked about ERP milestones and separation costs. Hanson and McMillan detailed ERP timelines and indicated that "we're expecting our free cash flows...to improve in 2026."
  • Steven James Valiquette, Mizuho Securities, inquired about the Ensemble partnership. Hanson described Ensemble as a leader in outsourcing revenue cycle management and said, "we wouldn't have done the partnership if we didn't think there was a real opportunity here."
  • Jennifer Reena Rabinowitz, Goldman Sachs, questioned the decision to raise guidance but maintain free cash flow. McMillan attributed this to a slow start in free cash flows and the expectation of improvement in the second half.
  • Brett Adam Fishbin, KeyBanc, requested color on Dental Solutions trends and upcoming launches. Hanson acknowledged "we really haven't seen a dramatic change" in patient trends but emphasized new products driving growth.
  • Lei Huang, Wells Fargo, asked about growth cadence in the second half. Hanson said, "you're going to see a bit of a pressure point in Q3 because we believe that's when we're going to get most of that order timing up."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts generally maintained a positive tone, expressing interest in the pace of growth, sustainability of improvements, and clarity around guidance adjustments. Questions focused on growth drivers, order timing, and cash flow.
  • Management’s remarks were confident and upbeat in both prepared statements and responses. Hanson repeatedly cited momentum, progress, and confidence in achieving targets, stating, "we are making consistent and repeatable progress in that value creation story."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed increased confidence, with management raising guidance and analysts probing for underlying business strength and future growth rates.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management raised both sales growth and EPS guidance in Q2, compared to confirming EPS and raising only revenue guidance in Q1.
  • Strategic focus on tuck-in M&A and innovation pipeline remained consistent, but Q2 included more detail on product launches and customer wins.
  • Order timing and SKU rationalization impacts were discussed in both quarters as key modeling considerations for growth rates.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from clarifying underlying growth in Q1 to examining the durability of improvements and sustainability of raised guidance in Q2.
  • Tone from both management and analysts was more confident in Q2, with increased discussion of execution, commercial momentum, and operational milestones.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted ongoing separation from 3M and ERP system implementations as challenges, noting "these types of implementations are not without challenges."
  • Tariffs remain a risk, but the estimated impact for 2025 was reduced, and mitigation strategies were emphasized.
  • SKU exits and order timing fluctuations present challenges to modeling growth.
  • Free cash flow was noted as an area to watch, with expectations for improvement as separation costs decline.

Final Takeaway

Solventum’s second quarter showcased increased momentum with raised guidance for both sales growth and EPS for 2025, backed by strong execution across business segments, successful product launches, and disciplined cost management. Management's confidence in achieving long-term growth and margin targets was underscored by progress in separation activities and strategic initiatives, while acknowledging ongoing challenges related to ERP implementation, tariffs, and integration activities. The company expects continued improvement as transformation efforts take hold and separation-related costs subside in the coming years.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
